text: In this video we want to show a technique for a fertility sparing cystectomy in case of left ovarian endometriosis cyst.

The surgeon uses cold scissors to carefully open the ovarian cyst, avoiding the use of energy devices to minimize the risk of ovarian failure.
The surgeon uses cold scissors to make an incision at the anti-mesial point of the ovary, beginning the process of opening the cyst.
The surgeon measures the ovarian endometriosis cyst at three centimeters and proceeds to remove the menstrual blood from within the cyst. The next step involves performing a cystoscopy.
The surgeon washes the ovarian endometriosis cyst and inspects the interior for any visible vegetation or abnormal tissue growth.
The surgeon visually confirms the absence of vegetation inside the cyst, preparing to initiate the cystectomy procedure.
The principle of the cystectomy is very simple.
